sources: Government of Tuvalu 2025-2026 National Budget Speech
Australia-Tuvalu Falepili Union Financial Commitment Framework

external_grants.name: Development Partner Budget Support (Australia, World Bank, ADB)

investment_returns.name: Tuvalu Trust Fund and CIF Distributions

2026.methodology: Projections are based on the balanced budget model adopted for the 2025-2026 fiscal year. Total revenue includes a 38% projected increase in fishing license fees due to new vessel agreements with Taiwan and China. All figures have been converted from AUD to USD at the projected March 2026 exchange rate (0.66). Spending is aligned with the 'Enabling Progress' strategy.

sources: Asian Development Bank (ADB) Pacific Economic Monitor 2025

fishing_licenses.name: Fishing Licenses and Vessel Access Fees

grants_external.name: External Development and Budget Support Grants

public_sector.name: Civil Service Salaries and General Administration

climate_adaptation.name: Climate Resilience and Coastal Protection Projects

2025.methodology: Figures represent the 2025 National Budget estimates converted to USD. Total Income reflects recurrent revenue plus committed external grants. Spending is calculated based on the 2025 Appropriations Act. Data accounts for strategic investments in climate adaptation as outlined in the FALEPILI Union agreement framework, adjusted for the USD exchange rate.

sources: Tuvalu Ministry of Finance 2024 National Budget
Tuvalu Central Statistics Division Economics Report

fishing_licenses.name: Fishing Licenses and Vessel Access Fees

2024.methodology: Data derived from the Tuvalu 2024 National Budget projected actuals. All values are converted from AUD to USD (0.66 rate). Total income includes recurrent revenue and general budget support. Spending is calculated based on allocated recurrent and capital expenditure for the 2024 fiscal cycle.
